name: Garbanzo Cassoulet
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak beans in water overnight.
2. Drain, then add enough water to a cooking pot so the beans are covered by 1-2 inches of water.
3. Stick cloves into the peeled onion (leave whole).
4. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pot along with the beans.
5. Bring to boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes.
6. Add carrots; simmer, covered, for an additional 20 minutes until beans and carrots are tender.
7. Drain, reserving cooking liquid. Discard onions, garlic, and ginger.
8. Set aside.
9. Heat oil in a skillet. Fry onion and garlic until the onion is translucent.
10. Stir in tomatoes. Add tomato sauce, reserved bean cooking liquid (or water), basil, and thyme.
11. Bring to boil; simmer, covered, for 45 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
12. Set aside.
13. Heat oil. Fry parsley and 1 cup bread crumbs for about 1 minute.
14. Stir often. Mix with the remaining 1 cup bread crumbs in a bowl.
15. Assemble: Preheat oven to 350°F.
16. Combine bean mixture and sauce in a deep casserole or baking dish.
17. Sprinkle 1/3 of the topping mixture on top.
18. Bake for 15 minutes, then push the topping layer down with the back of a spoon and sprinkle another 1/3 of topping on.
19. Bake another 15 minutes, repeat the procedure, and add the remaining topping.
20. Bake an additional 10 minutes, then place under broiler for 5 minutes or until lightly browned on top.
